mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer spending and overall economic health, which directly supports corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The correlation between monthly RSAFS growth and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is consistently negative or statistically insignificant over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is maintained above a 4.5% threshold, it signals a restrictive monetary policy stance aimed at cooling an overheating economy, which typically dampens corporate profitability and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where the FEDFUNDS rate is above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of a low unemployment rate (UNRATE below 5.0%) and strong payroll growth (PAYEMS 3-month growth > 0.1%) indicates an overheating labor market, which often precedes wage inflation and subsequent monetary tightening, negatively impacting equity valuations.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive or flat when both conditions are met, or the interaction term between UNRATE and PAYEMS shows no predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: A significant rebound in housing starts (HOUST 3-month average growth > 5%) following a period of decline signals renewed confidence in the economy and increased investment, which typically translates into broader economic growth and positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: During periods identified as housing recovery, S&P 500 20-day returns do not show a statistically significant positive bias (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects robust manufacturing and supply-side activity, indicating healthy corporate earnings and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: The correlation between monthly INDPRO growth and S&P 500 5-day forward returns is consistently negative or zero over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL 12-month % change > 3.0%) combined with a rapidly rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0.25%) indicates aggressive monetary tightening to combat price pressures, which typically leads to an economic slowdown and negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances where both high inflation and rising rates are present, or the interaction term lacks statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points in 3 months) signals a weakening labor market and potential economic contraction, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, thus driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where the unemployment rate rises sharply by 0.3pp or more over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Robust GDP growth (GDPC1) signifies a strong overall economy, which generally translates into higher corporate revenues and profits, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth consistently fails to predict positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon across multiple expansionary periods, with a Rank IC below 0.03.
